# Items 5/6/12 become HARD GATES, not audit items:

#   structure_gate:     body line count <= 60 (or <=15 for main theorems).

#   naming_gate:        no forbidden patterns in declaration name:

#                       no `multipass_`, no `_aux\d+` numeric suffix.
#                       Rename per mathlib convention: describe what is PROVED.
#   line_packing_gate:  hypothesis lines packed to ~100 chars where possible.
#                       Multiple hypotheses per line is the norm, not the exception.
#
# === Audit-only items (report and address, deferral allowed) ===
#   1 (LINT), 3 (SET_OPTION), 4 (SIMP SQUEEZE), 7 (BY PLACEMENT), 8 (FORMAT),
#   9 (COMMENTS), 10 (DOCSTRING), 11 (VISIBILITY), 14 (JUNK DEF), 15 (EXISTS LEMMA),

#   REJECTED, re-dispatch with explicit pack instructions.
description: The /cleanup Phase-4 worker template has an escape valve that prevents quality enforcement on large files. Audit items 5 (NAMING), 6 (LINE PACKING), and 12 (STRUCTURE) are framed as report-or-defer, not pass-fail gates. On small files this is invisible (workers fix because there's little to defer); on large files (1000+ lines, many specialised declarations) workers routinely defer, leaving bad names, long proofs, and sparse hypothesis blocks in place. Specific failure modes observed: (1) 'signature locked' misread — workers refuse to extract `private` helpers ABOVE the declaration, claiming the signature-protection rule blocks it; the rule only blocks changing the declaration's OWN signature. (2) NAMING audit reports 'existing convention preserved' without ever proposing a rename, even when names encode internal scheme numbers (4_6_5_, m6_2_, multipass_) instead of mathematical content. (3) LINE PACKING reports 'ok' without packing one-per-line hypothesis blocks that could fit on shared lines up to 100 chars. The fix: convert items 5, 6, 12 to hard pass-fail gates with explicit rejection criteria. Re-dispatch workers whose runs violate any gate, feeding the violation back as the worker's prompt. Disambiguate 'signature locked' to clarify that helpers placed above the declaration are not signature changes. This was observed in a /cleanup run on an 8286-line modular forms file across ~15 sequential workers, where structure/naming/packing defects accumulated visibly while every worker reported 'Gates: pass'.

description: For the char-agnostic recursive old/new decomposition (Atkin-Lehner-Li), the clean Lean structure is two phases: Phase 1 proves cuspFormsOld ≤ span{V_l(new at proper levels)} by strong induction on N (split each generator V_d(g) into V_d(oldPart g)+V_d(newPart g); old piece descends by IH and folds via levelRaise associativity V_d∘V_e=V_{de}). Phase 2 handles the character via an isotypic-collapse lemma (iSupIndep.disjoint_biSup drops non-χ pieces) plus DirichletCharacter.conductor_dvd_of_mem_conductorSet to get m_χ ∣ M. Avoids per-generator charSpace tracking in the span_induction motive.

Miyake 4.6.12 T010 `oldPart_eq_zero_of_shared_eigenvalues` is mis-stated: its `h_eig` hypothesis is the IMPLICATION `∀ lam, (T_n g_old = lam • g_old) → f.eigenvalue n = lam`, which is VACUOUSLY satisfied when g_old is not a T_n-eigenform (e.g. a nonzero sum of two level-raised newforms with distinct eigensystems). Miyake's g⁽²⁾ is genuinely a T(n)-eigenform with eigenvalue aₙ (Lemma 4.6.10), so the faithful encoding must ASSERT `T_n g_old = f.eigenvalue n • g_old` unconditionally, not as a vacuous implication. As stated the theorem is unprovable (nonzero non-eigenform counterexamples).
math_area: modular forms / Hecke theory / newform theory

description: Miyake 4.6.12 step (ii) descent contradiction needs the TRIVIAL inclusion (l=1, id) of a level-M newform h into level N to lie in the old space, so that `h − c₁'·f` (matching f's coprime coefficients) is old by Thm 4.6.8 and hence f∈old. But the project's `cuspFormsOld N k` (Newforms/Basic.lean) deliberately uses ONLY levelRaise generators with d>1 (excludes the d=1 trivial inclusion, per the line-120 comment, to avoid collapsing to ⊤). The trivial inclusion lives in the SEPARATE `cuspFormsOldExtended`. Level-raised pieces V_l(h) (l>1) have a₁=0 and vanishing coprime coefficients, so they do NOT give the coefficient-matching `h − c₁'·f` argument. Also `Newform.conductor` is a placeholder (:= N), so there is no real conductor-newform correspondence to force M=N. Hence T010/T013 descent is NOT dischargeable from the listed proven leaves; it needs either a cuspFormsOld↔cuspFormsOldExtended bridge or genuine cross-level newform uniqueness.
math_area: modular forms / Hecke theory / newform old/new decomposition

description: Authoring a thematic leanblueprint on a large project. (1) BOOTSTRAP: `leanblueprint new` (NOT `init`) is fully interactive with no flags; the bundled copier template lives at site-packages/leanblueprint/templates/. To scaffold non-interactively, copy blueprint.sty, extra_styles.css, latexmkrc and macros/{common,web,print}.tex verbatim, then write web.tex/print.tex/plastex.cfg/content.tex substituting the copier vars (documentclass=report, paper=a4paper, toc_depth=3, split_level=0, github/home/dochome from the git remote). PDF uses macros/print.tex which dummies out \lean/\uses/\leanok; web uses \usepackage[showmore,dep_graph]{blueprint} via the plastex leanblueprint plugin. (2) FAN-OUT: for a curated thematic scope, one Agent per CHAPTER (not per decl) works well IF each worker gets an explicit per-row checklist (label + fully-qualified \lean name + source line + math identity) AND a GLOBAL LABEL MAP so cross-chapter \uses{} resolve by construction; this defeats the skip failure-mode while letting all chapters run in parallel (different files => no write race). (3) CAVEATS: the template loads neither cleveref (use \ref not \cref) nor a .bib (use literal [Sh, Thm 3.24] text, never \cite). (4) VERIFY without checkdecls: real `leanblueprint checkdecls` needs a `require checkdecls` in the lakefile + full build; instead grep each \lean leaf for its declaration and run lean_verify on a sample. Both `leanblueprint pdf` and `leanblueprint web` then compiled clean (xelatex+plastex+dot).

learning: Verso migration mechanics: :::lemma_ needs trailing underscore; bold is single asterisk (** lints); citation brackets must be escaped \[Sh, §3.1\]; no title:= attr (bold lead-in instead); uses=edge vs bpref=link-only — several workers downgraded \uses to bpref and needed patching; proof attaches by matching statement label.

Validate (lean := X) refs by lake env lean EXIT CODE on a mass-import #check file, not by grepping for unknownIdentifier — a broken transitive import aborts elaboration and greps read as clean. Also: concurrent worker lake builds race on .lake/build oleans; forbid builds in worker prompts and build once at the end.

Dead-code reachability sweeps delete exactly the blueprint-worthy decls (headline LEAF theorems nothing imports: Shimura 3.24 endpoints, thm 3.35, R_p_isPolynomialRing, charSpace directSum, weight<12 vanishing). Restoration: git show <pre-sweep>:file > file works when post-sweep diffs are deletion-only (verify +0 added lines first). Future sweeps should whitelist decls referenced in Chapters/*.lean.

description: For a one-line MonoidHom.comp wrapper def whose body composes with a noncomputable project-local map, the `noncomputable` marker is genuine (cannot be dropped), but `[NeZero N]` was vestigial and removed via `omit [NeZero N] in`, and `↥(Gamma0 N)` was simplified to `Gamma0 N` (Subgroup→Sort coercion is automatic in binder positions). Added an `@[simp] _apply` lemma (`= rfl`) so downstream stops unfolding the def with `simp [gamma0NebentypusChar]`.
before_code:
noncomputable def gamma0NebentypusChar (χ : (ZMod N)ˣ →* ℂˣ) : ↥(Gamma0 N) →* ℂˣ :=
  χ.comp (Gamma0MapUnits (N := N))
after_code:
omit [NeZero N] in
noncomputable def gamma0NebentypusChar (χ : (ZMod N)ˣ →* ℂˣ) : Gamma0 N →* ℂˣ :=
  χ.comp (Gamma0MapUnits (N := N))

omit [NeZero N] in
@[simp] lemma gamma0NebentypusChar_apply (χ : (ZMod N)ˣ →* ℂˣ) (g : Gamma0 N) :
    gamma0NebentypusChar χ g = χ (Gamma0MapUnits g) := rfl

description: When `convert h_K using 1` is used to close a goal whose only difference from h_K is coercion distribution (Subgroup.coe_mul / Subgroup.coe_inv), add those lemmas to the preceding `simp only` call at h_K instead. This eliminates the convert entirely and makes the proof a clean `exact`. Pattern: `simp only [...] at h_K; exact ... (by convert h_K using 1)` → `simp only [..., Subgroup.coe_mul, Subgroup.coe_inv] at h_K; exact ... h_K`.

Injectivity of a bespoke "left-translate then re-wrap" map i ↦ ⟦σ * i.out⟧ on a QuotientGroup decompQuot collapses from a 23-line by_contra/coset-disjointness argument to 4 lines by working directly with the leftRel relation: rw the goal into leftRel form via Quotient.out_eq + Quotient.eq'' + QuotientGroup.leftRel_apply, then `convert (leftRel_apply.mp (Quotient.exact h)) using 1` and discharge the single element-equality goal with `Subtype.ext (by simp only [Subgroup.coe_mul, InvMemClass.coe_inv]; group)` — the σ cancels under `group`. The coset-disjointness route (leftCoset_eq_of_not_disjoint + not_disjoint_iff + two singleton-mul witnesses) was entirely unnecessary.
before_code:
intro i₁ i₂ h
  simp only [gamma0LeftMulQuot] at h
  by_contra hne
  have h_K := QuotientGroup.leftRel_apply.mp (Quotient.exact h)
  simp only [Subgroup.mem_subgroupOf] at h_K
  have h_mem : (rep D)⁻¹ * ((i₁.out)⁻¹ * i₂.out) * (rep D) ∈ H := by
    rw [Subgroup.mem_pointwise_smul_iff_inv_smul_mem, ConjAct.smul_def] at h_K
    simp only [...]; convert h_K using 1; simp [...]; group
  exact decompQuot_coset_diff _ _ i₁ i₂ hne
    (leftCoset_eq_of_not_disjoint _ _ _ (by rw [Set.not_disjoint_iff];
after_code:
intro i₁ i₂ h
  rw [← Quotient.out_eq i₁, ← Quotient.out_eq i₂, Quotient.eq'', QuotientGroup.leftRel_apply]
  convert QuotientGroup.leftRel_apply.mp (Quotient.exact h) using 1
  exact Subtype.ext (by simp only [Subgroup.coe_mul, InvMemClass.coe_inv]; group)

description: A `Nat.card_eq_finsetCard` + `Nat.card_congr` + hand-built `Equiv` (toFun/invFun/left_inv/right_inv) proving `s.card = Nat.card {x | p x}` collapses to one `Nat.subtype_card` call once you supply the membership iff `∀ x, x ∈ s ↔ p x`. Saved 10 body lines.
before_code:
rw [← Nat.card_eq_finsetCard]
apply Nat.card_congr
exact { toFun := fun ⟨p, hp⟩ ↦ ⟨p, by ...⟩
        invFun := fun ⟨p, hp_rc⟩ ↦ ⟨p, by ...⟩
        left_inv := fun ⟨_, _⟩ ↦ rfl
        right_inv := fun ⟨_, _⟩ ↦ rfl }
after_code:
refine (Nat.subtype_card _ fun p ↦ ?_).symm
simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and]
refine ⟨fun ⟨hmap, hq⟩ ↦ hq ▸ h_coset_eq p hmap, fun hp_rc ↦ ?_⟩
have hmap := gamma0_mulMap_eq_of_rightCoset D₁ D₂ D p q hp_rc
refine ⟨hmap, byContradiction fun hne ↦ decompQuot_coset_diff ...⟩
